chinese: adj. 滿意的，滿足的 (happy, satisfied)
example: Jessie was [content] with her selfie and decided to post it online.

chinese: adv. 然而，儘管如此 (despite what has just been mentioned)
example: Gina had a bad headache this morning. [Nevertheless], she still went to work.

chinese: n. [C] 概念，觀念 (an idea of something)
example: The child is too young to understand the [concept] of death.

chinese: vt. 發……的音，發音 (to make the sound of a word)
example: This one-year-old baby can only [pronounce] "papa" and "mama" now.
Do you know the correct [pronunciation] of the store's name "IKEA"?

chinese: n. [C][U] 氣氛，氛圍 (the feeling that a place gives someone)
example: This coffee shop has a friendly [atmosphere] because of its design and furniture.

chinese: adj. 著迷的 (thinking something is very interesting)
example: The students are [fascinated] to see how the AI robot could answer questions.
notes: fascination with/for sb/sth：對某人/某事感興趣
be fascinated to V (see/hear/learn)：對於看到/聽到/學習某事感到著迷

example: The idea of traveling around the world has always [fascinated] me.

chinese: vi.; vt. 嘆氣，嘆息 (to breathe out and make a long sound)
example: When Daniel's dog messed up the room again, he [sighed] deeply and began to clean up.
Brian breathed a [sigh] of relief when he heard his parents were not injured in the car accident.
notes: relief (n.)：放鬆、鬆口氣
give / let out a sigh：發出嘆息

example: I'm [roughly] the same age as Ivy. She is only a few months younger than I am.
notes: roughly speaking：大致上來說
roughly 亦有：粗魯地、粗暴地、粗糙地、崎嶇不平地

chinese: adj. 約略的；惡劣的、猛烈的、粗糙的、令人難受的、刺耳的
example: Judy and I haven't decided what to do tonight. We only have some [rough] ideas like shopping or watching movies.

chinese: adj. 被動的，消極的 (accepting something without trying to change)
example: Helen plays a [passive] role on the team. She doesn't join in the discussions at all.
notes: play a role of...：扮演...角色
join + 組織、團隊、夥伴
join in + 活動 (如果很清楚受詞，直接說 join in)

chinese: vt. 遭遇，遇到 (to experience something unpleasant)
example: Robin [encountered] a great number of difficulties when he was looking for his first job.
Stacy felt a sense of [frustration] when she failed to get her dream job.
notes: be/feel frustrated at/with + N/Ving：對...感到沮喪

example: The negative comments [frustrated] him a lot.
Mother Teresa [devoted] her whole life to helping poor people.
notes: dedicate to N/Ving
devote self to sb/sth：貢獻給某人/某事
devotion (n.)：忠誠、摯愛、奉獻

example: Gretchen is a [devoted] fan of Wes Anderson.
Sam won three movie tickets. He kept one for himself and gave the [remaining] tickets to his friends.
notes: sth remains to be done：待辦事宜/尚待完成

chinese: vi. 繼續存在；保持不變
example: Very few fish [remain] in the river now due to the serious water pollution.
notes: remains (n.)：剩餘物、遺跡

example: Lisa is so [stubborn] that she cannot accept what other people say.
notes: as stubborn as a mule：倔強得像頭驢子（非常固執）
so + adj/adv + that + S + V：如此...以至於...
such + N + that + S + V

example: Ryan believes in all the star sign stuff. He often talks about how it influences people's behavior, careers, love lives, and so on.
notes: stuff (vt.)：填滿
stuff A with B：用 B 裝滿 A
Danish pastry：丹麥麵包
spill the beans/tea：洩漏消息
cry over spilled milk：覆水難收
(v.) 選拔、適用
try sth out on sb：拿某人試試看（某物）
+ N/Ving
= to put it another way = that is to say = that is = namely
= make every effort to V
